body_txt: Sometimes, the most powerful fighter will not be equipped with such Stock Superpowers as super-strength, or Nigh-Invulnerability . Instead, they have the power to see into the future. Why is this useful? Because it lets you know their next move, and plan accordingly. Being able to see even a few seconds into the future can be a huge advantage in a fight and can let someone get the edge over opponents that might have more skill or experience and just the shock of having their moves foreseen can cause them to slip up and make mistakes. This power is also one of the few ways to react to Super-Speed without matching it in kind. However, although it can give a big advantage in a fight, there are generally two logical weaknesses that can let people overcome this ability. The first is by simply being so much stronger and faster than your opponent that they can't react or defend against your attacks. After all, it doesn't matter if your opponent knows what you're going to do next if they physically can't stop you or protect themselves from you. The second is setting up a scenario that's basically the same as the first by simply outlasting your opponent and waiting for them to be too injured or tired to keep up with you and unable to defend against you. For the second strategy, having Super-Toughness or being Nigh-Invulnerable would be a must because it doesn't matter if they can keep hitting you if they don't have the power to hurt you. In some instances you could also hit them with something that can't be avoided because they won't have time to get outside of the radius before it arrives. This typically requires knowing that they have the power and being able to plan around it. For example, if they can see five minutes into the future, using an invisible, odorless Deadly Gas that kills its victims about ten minutes after exposure. Another possible counter is to base your own actions on what your opponent does. This means that if they try to react based on their vision of the future, what actually happens will always differ from what they predicted because you change your own reaction based on their reaction. For example, if you're playing Rock Paper Scissors where your opponent goes first, you can always win, even if they can see the future. Why this doesn't muddle up their ability to know what you're doing now can be chalked up to the Rule of Cool . Alternately, it can be Hand Waved by saying that they're not looking that far into the future, or by noting their brain can keep up with both . ( obviously, being able to feel/perceive the opponent's intent solves this: you know their current-configuration, AND you know their intent, their next-action : ) Often accompanied by Spider-Sense , which warns the user of vague danger instead of predicting specific attacks. In certain stories, usually the mark of the Psychic Assassin variant of the Psychic Operative . Compare Kung-Fu Clairvoyance where the hero seems to have this, but it's not canonically one of their abilities. Compare also with Prescience by Analysis , which is when a character can predict the future more broadly using their analytical abilities and intelligence. Contrast with Super-Reflexes , which is simply being able to quickly react to attacks as they happen, not before. Compare and contrast Memorized the Whole Process , where this is result of constantly studying the opponent. Not to be confused for Calling Your Attacks , which is about calling out the names of your own attacks as you do them.
